15-progressive-tasks.notes: ignore_error on step.db_exec added to engine; parse_headers on step.request_parse added; auth enforcement via header check + conditional routing; INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotent category creation.

16-progressive-orders.notes: Fixed {{ steps.fetch.row.status }} template syntax (missing leading dot) in cancel-order pipeline.

17-full-lifecycle.notes: Fixed: conditional uses steps.fetch.found=false for 404; {{ .tag }} not {{ .query.tag }} for query params; COALESCE+NULLIF+default for partial updates; INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotent contact creation.

18-template-validation.notes: Skips: plugin/ui-plugin templates have no workflow.yaml (expected). template/contract/compat/ui sub-commands return exit 1 on --help so cmd_available returns false; those test branches skipped.

19-version-contracts.notes: Skips: wfctl contract --help exits 1 so cmd_available returns false; contract/compare tests skipped. Note: contract compare subcommand does not exist (use contract test -baseline instead). Phase 5 OpenAPI comparison fully passes.

20-auth-service.notes: Uses auth.jwt native handlers with allowRegistration: true for open self-registration. v0.2.12 adds static.fileserver route fix. UI generated via wfctl api extract + wfctl ui scaffold from OpenAPI spec. Playwright QA: 8/8 browser tests passed.

21-payment-service.notes: Payment lifecycle: pending→captured→refunded. Webhook via step.http_call on capture. UI generated via wfctl api extract + wfctl ui scaffold. Playwright QA: 26/30 browser tests passed (4 capture failures are backend state machine edge cases). Test 14 verifies DB state via GET after capture.

22-ecommerce-app.notes: Full cross-service integration passing. Flow: register user (auth-service) → create order (calls payment-service) → capture payment → webhook updates order status to paid. UI generated via wfctl api extract + wfctl ui scaffold. Playwright QA: 9/9 browser integration tests passed.

23-nosql-datastore.notes: NoSQL data store modules (DynamoDB, MongoDB, Redis) with mock backends. CRUD lifecycle via pipeline steps.

24-artifact-store.notes: Artifact store with filesystem backend. Upload, download, list, delete lifecycle via pipeline steps.

25-cloud-account.notes: Cloud account credential resolution for AWS with mock backend. Validates credential retrieval via pipeline step.

26-config-to-binary.notes: Config-to-binary build step. Takes workflow config YAML and builds a standalone Go binary.

27-platform-kubernetes.notes: Platform Kubernetes module with kind backend. Real Workflow HTTP app drives plan/apply/status/destroy lifecycle.

28-iac-pipeline.notes: Real Workflow HTTP app drives iac.state filesystem persistence and generic IaC lifecycle steps: plan → apply → status → drift-detect → destroy. No real cluster required (kind backend).

29-gitlab-ci.notes: Workflow app-boundary proof for workflow-plugin-gitlab as an external plugin. Builds/installs the plugin, starts workflow-server, and drives webhook parsing plus pipeline/MR/comment flows through HTTP with caller-supplied request bodies and a module-backed mock GitLab client.

30-ecs-fargate.notes: Platform ECS/Fargate module. Plan/apply/status/destroy lifecycle with mock backend. Task definitions, services, networking config.

31-platform-networking.notes: Workflow app-boundary proof for platform networking. Builds/installs workflow-plugin-aws as an external plugin, starts workflow-server, and drives caller-supplied VPC/firewall specs and refs through plan/apply/status/destroy HTTP APIs with AWS mock mode and no live credentials.

32-platform-dns.notes: Workflow app-boundary proof for platform DNS. Starts workflow-server, drives mock platform.dns plan/apply/status through HTTP APIs, and asserts DNS zone and record state without live provider credentials.

33-apigateway-autoscaling.notes: Workflow app-boundary proof for API Gateway and autoscaling. Starts workflow-server, installs workflow-plugin-aws in mock mode, and drives caller-supplied infra.api_gateway and infra.autoscaling_group specs/refs through plan/apply/status/destroy HTTP APIs without live AWS credentials.

34-app-container.notes: App container deployment abstraction. Deploy/status/rollback lifecycle using mock backend. k8s and ECS backends.

35-multi-cloud-accounts.notes: GCP and Azure credential types added to cloud.account module. Service account, managed identity, workload identity support.

36-argo-workflows.notes: Argo Workflows integration. Pipeline-to-DAG translation, submit/status/logs/delete lifecycle. Mock backend. Unit tests pass; HTTP integration tests have expected failures.

37-aws-codebuild.notes: AWS CodeBuild plugin. Project/build lifecycle, buildspec generation from pipeline config. Mock backend. Unit tests pass; HTTP integration tests have expected failures.

38-policy-engine.notes: External policy engine adapter. OPA/Cedar backends with evaluate/load/list/test steps. Mock backend. Unit tests pass; HTTP integration tests have expected failures.

39-distributed-tracing.notes: Distributed tracing propagation. HTTP/Kafka/EventBridge/Webhook propagators, span creation, trace linking. Unit tests pass; HTTP integration tests have expected failures.

40-multi-region.notes: Multi-region tenant deployment. Deploy/promote/failover/status/weight/sync lifecycle. Failover state machine. Unit tests pass; HTTP integration tests have expected failures.

41-plugin-marketplace.notes: Plugin marketplace. Search/detail/install/uninstall/update steps with mock registry backend. Unit tests pass; HTTP integration tests have expected failures.

42-digitalocean.notes: DigitalOcean cloud provider. DOKS, VPC/firewalls, DNS, App Platform with real godo client + mock backends. Unit tests pass; HTTP integration tests have expected failures.

43-agent-plugin-basic.notes: Agent plugin basic integration. agent.provider (mock), step.agent_execute, step.provider_models. Requires workflow-server:agent-local image with workflow-plugin-agent registered.

44-agent-self-healing.notes: Scripted multi-turn agent simulating self-healing infrastructure. test provider in scripted mode with 6-step CrashLoopBackOff remediation sequence. Tests iteration counting and max_iterations enforcement. Requires workflow-server:agent-local image.

45-agent-operator-mode.notes: Manual QA only. test provider in HTTP mode routes agent turns to an external Claude operator process. See README.md for setup. Requires workflow-server:agent-local image.

49-security-scanning.notes: Uses built-in security.scanner module in mock mode. Step types: step.scan_sast, step.scan_container, step.scan_deps.

50-actor-model.notes: Exercises goakt actor model: actor.system, actor.pool (auto-managed + permanent), step.actor_ask, step.actor_send. Order lifecycle with stateful actors + round-robin worker pool.

52-monday-integration.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-monday step types against a mock monday.com GraphQL API server. Validates board/item/group CRUD and generic queries.

53-turnio-integration.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-turnio step types against a mock turn.io API server. Validates WhatsApp messaging, contacts, templates, flows, and rate limit tracking.

59-discord-messaging.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-discord step types against a mock Discord API server on :19059. Validates send message, send embed, add reaction, and create thread operations.

60-slack-messaging.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-slack step types against a mock Slack API server on :19060. Validates send message, send blocks, thread reply, and set topic operations.

61-teams-messaging.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-teams step types against a mock Microsoft Graph API server on :19061. Validates send message, send adaptive card, reply message, and create channel operations.

62-cross-platform-messaging.notes: Cross-platform broadcast pipeline using Discord, Slack, and Teams plugins simultaneously. Mock servers on :19062 (Discord), :19063 (Slack), :19064 (Teams). Tests broadcast delivery, per-platform message ID collection, and Discord-to-Slack relay with [Discord] prefix.

64-iac-do-basic.notes: Config-validation scenario for a basic DigitalOcean IaC stack. Validates iac.provider (provider: digitalocean), iac.state (backend: memory), infra.vpc, infra.database (postgres 16), and infra.container_service (nginx:latest, 2 replicas). No live cloud API calls.

65-iac-aws-basic.notes: Config-validation scenario for a basic AWS IaC stack. Validates iac.provider (provider: aws), iac.state (backend: memory), infra.vpc, infra.database (RDS postgres 16), and infra.container_service (ECS Fargate, nginx:latest, 2 replicas). No live cloud API calls.

66-iac-multi-cloud.notes: Config-validation scenario for provider-agnostic IaC. The same config targets aws or digitalocean via IAC_PROVIDER env var. Validates that wfctl validate passes for both providers and that all infra modules reference a single shared cloud-provider module.

67-iac-tofu-generate.notes: Config-validation scenario for the tofu.generator plugin. Validates that step.tofu_generate steps produce vpc.tf, database.tf, and ecs.tf output file references. Also tests step.tofu_validate and step.tofu_plan pipeline wiring. No live Tofu execution.

68-ci-generator.notes: Config-validation scenario for the ci.generator plugin. Validates two generator modules (provider: github, provider: gitlab), step.ci_generate steps producing .github/workflows/ci.yml and .gitlab-ci.yml, step.ci_validate, and step.ci_diff. No live CI execution.

69-iac-deployment-rolling.notes: Config-validation scenario for rolling deployments. Validates infra.container_service with rollingUpdate config (maxSurge, maxUnavailable, healthCheckPath), step.deploy_rolling referencing the service, and step.deploy_verify. iac.provider: digitalocean, iac.state: memory.

70-iac-deployment-blue-green.notes: Config-validation scenario for blue-green deployments. Validates two infra.container_service modules (blue + green), infra.load_balancer with health check, step.deploy_blue_green with blueService/greenService/loadBalancer references, and a rollback pipeline. iac.provider: aws.

71-iac-deployment-canary.notes: Config-validation scenario for canary deployments. Validates stable + canary infra.container_service modules, step.deploy_canary with 3 stages (5%/25%/100%) and metric gates (error_rate, p99_latency_ms), plus canary-promote and canary-abort pipelines. iac.provider: gcp.

72-iac-state-backends.notes: Config-validation scenario testing all 6 iac.state backend types: memory, filesystem (path), postgres (connectionString), gcs (bucket+prefix), azure_blob (storageAccount+container), s3 (bucket+region+encrypt). Each backend has a dedicated plan pipeline.

73-iac-sizing-tiers.notes: Config-validation scenario testing all 5 sizing tiers (xs/s/m/l/xl) for infra.database and infra.container_service. Also validates resource hint overrides (cpu, memory, storage) on the xl tier. iac.provider: aws, iac.state: memory.

74-iac-full-stack.notes: Config-validation scenario exercising all 13 infra.* module types: vpc, database, cache, container_service, load_balancer, dns, registry, firewall, iam_role, storage, certificate, cdn, secret. Full production-grade AWS stack. iac.provider: aws, iac.state: memory.

75-deployment-pipeline.notes: Config-validation scenario for end-to-end CI/CD pipeline: step.iac_plan → step.iac_apply → step.container_build → step.deploy_rolling → step.deploy_verify. Includes infra.registry and a rollback pipeline. iac.provider: digitalocean.

76-tofu-generate-pipeline.notes: Config-validation scenario for HCL generation across 4 providers: aws (hashicorp/aws ~>5.0), gcp (hashicorp/google ~>5.0), azure (hashicorp/azurerm ~>3.0), digitalocean (digitalocean/digitalocean ~>2.0). One tofu.generator and per-cloud step.iac_generate_hcl steps plus generate-all pipeline.

77-ci-generate-multi-platform.notes: Config-validation scenario for generating GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/ci.yml) and GitLab CI (.gitlab-ci.yml) from a single config. Two ci.generator modules coexist, step.ci_generate for both platforms, step.ci_validate, step.ci_diff. Includes generate-all and diff-ci pipelines.

78-infra-module-wiring.notes: Config-validation scenario testing the iac.provider → infra.* → step.iac_* dependency chain. All infra modules (vpc, database, container_service) explicitly reference provider: aws-provider; all IaC steps reference state_store: iac-state. Validates plan-all, apply-all, status-check pipelines.

63-twilio-integration.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-twilio step types against a mock Twilio API server. Validates SMS/MMS sending, message listing, verification, call creation, and phone lookup.

51-bmw-iac.notes: BMW IaC scenario using DigitalOcean mock providers. Exercises plan/apply/status/drift/destroy lifecycle with iac.state filesystem backend.

54-okta-integration.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-okta step types against a mock Okta API server. Validates user CRUD, groups, and application listing.

55-datadog-integration.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-datadog step types against a mock Datadog API server. Validates metrics, events, monitors, and dashboards.

56-launchdarkly-integration.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-launchdarkly step types against a mock LaunchDarkly API server. Validates flag evaluation, toggle, and project listing.

57-salesforce-integration.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-salesforce step types against a mock Salesforce API server. Validates lead/opportunity/contact CRUD and SOQL queries.

58-openlms-integration.notes: Tests workflow-plugin-openlms step types against a mock Moodle Web Services server. Validates course/user enrollment operations.

79-data-cdc-pipeline.notes: Config-validation scenario testing CDC pipeline: PostgreSQL WAL capture via Bento, multi-tenant schema isolation (schema_per_tenant), Kafka output. Validates cdc.source, data.tenancy, messaging.kafka modules and all 6 CDC step types.

80-data-lakehouse-pipeline.notes: Config-validation scenario testing lakehouse ingestion: Iceberg REST catalog, Parquet write with quality gates, schema registry BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE compatibility. Validates catalog.iceberg, lakehouse.table, quality.checks, catalog.schema_registry modules and all 10 step types.

81-data-timeseries-analytics.notes: Config-validation scenario testing time-series analytics: InfluxDB + ClickHouse dual write, hourly downsampling (cron), z-score anomaly detection (cron), Druid Kafka supervisor for real-time OLAP. Validates all 3 TS modules and 8 step types.

82-data-graph-catalog.notes: Config-validation scenario testing knowledge graph + data catalog: Neo4j entity extraction and graph building, DataHub lineage registration, OpenMetadata indexing, sequential schema migrations with backup. Validates 4 modules and 11 step types.

83-enterprise-lead-enrichment.notes: AI-powered lead scoring with CRM sync and human-in-the-loop approval. Validates CRM adapter, approval state machine, and cross-plugin composition with sidecar containers.

85-self-improving-api.notes: Self-improving API scenario. AI agent (Ollama + Gemma 4) improves a SQLite task CRUD API by adding FTS5 search, cursor-based pagination, rate limiting, and structured logging. Validates agent.guardrails, step.agent_execute, step.blackboard_post, and hot_reload deploy strategy via Docker Compose.

86-self-extending-mcp.notes: Self-extending MCP tooling scenario. Agent creates task_analytics and task_forecast as mcp_tool trigger pipelines, uses them iteratively. Validates mcp:self_improve:* permission, guardrails, and two-iteration tool chain. Real Ollama + Gemma 4 via Docker Compose.

87-autonomous-agile-agent.notes: Autonomous agile agent scenario. Agent audits, plans, deploys, and verifies up to 5 iterations without human direction. Full loop: audit → plan → validate → deploy → verify → git_commit. Real Ollama + Gemma 4 via Docker Compose.

88-iac-dns-replay-migration.notes: Offline replay scenario for DNS/IaC import and migration safety. Validates sanitized Cloudflare, DigitalOcean, Namecheap, Hover, AWS, Azure, and GCP snapshots without provider accounts.

89-dns-import-export-roundtrip.notes: Drives wfctl infra import-all against the shared dns-stub plugin (scenarios/lib/dns-stub-plugin), then asserts a plan against the same config emits the canonical zero-action `No changes` message. Validates IaCProviderEnumerator.EnumerateAll + IaCProvider.Import + ComputePlan roundtrip without provider credentials.

90-admin-tailnet-demo.notes: Docker Compose app/admin demo with auth-gated admin portal, dynamic auth provider descriptor catalog, authz RBAC/ABAC/ReBAC UI, Ory Keto enforcement, and optional Tailscale sidecar.

91-dns-delegation.notes: Parent zone at stub-A delegates child.example.test to stub-B via NS records; child zone served at stub-B. Single `wfctl infra apply` reconciles both providers; subsequent imports assert the delegation survives via .applied_config.records[] jq matchers.

92-infra-admin-demo.notes: Infra-admin migration demo (workflow v0.70.0): the deleted engine infra.admin module is replaced by step.iac_provider_* pipeline steps + an EXTERNAL stub-iac-provider gRPC plugin (registered via WiringHook) + the external workflow-plugin-infra SPA (type:infra.admin, served at /admin/infra). Real-stack boot: 3 external plugins. 28 shell + 25 Playwright assertions: live catalog dropdowns, plan/desired_hash, two-phase apply, sandbox git commit, server-side authz (unauth 401, viewer 403, operator allowed), secrets metadata-only, CSRF.

93-dns-cross-provider-transfer.notes: Applies the same DNS record set against two stub IaCProvider instances (stub-A, stub-B) representing distinct clouds, imports both states, and asserts (type, name, data, ttl) parity per a per-(provider, record_type, field) lossiness charter. NS records excluded from the matrix. Renumbered from 90 to free slot 93.

94-iac-namecheap-dns.notes: Config-validation scenario for a Namecheap-managed DNS zone via workflow IaC. Exercises iac.provider.namecheap + infra.dns resource type. No live cloud API calls. Renumbered from 70 to free slot 94.

95-iac-hover-dns.notes: Config-validation scenario for a Hover-managed DNS zone via workflow IaC. Hover has no official API; the plugin mimics the browser-side username+password+TOTP login flow. No live Hover API calls. Renumbered from 71 to free slot 95.

96-iac-dyndns-multiprovider.notes: Config-validation scenario for the infra.dyndns module backed by multiple DNS providers in a single config (DigitalOcean + Namecheap + Hover). Demonstrates the provider-agnostic dyndns shape. No live cloud or DNS API calls. Renumbered from 72 to free slot 96.

97-secrets-wizard-noninteractive.notes: Showcases wfctl secrets setup --non-interactive --from-env against a file-backed store. Asserts set/unset JSON status, --skip-existing no-op, and that the audit JSONL never contains raw secret values (value-never-leaked).

98-ci-generate-smart.notes: Showcases wfctl ci plan --out plan.json (platform-neutral CIPlan JSON with secrets+warnings) then wfctl ci generate --from-plan producing a .github/workflows/*.yml with secret env wiring, plugin install step, functional migration step (wfctl migrations up — post-PR4 fix; asserts no obsolete --phase migrate), and smoke-test job.

99-ci-generate-edit-existing.notes: Showcases wfctl ci generate --diff --exit-code drift detection: exits 1 with diff output when the on-disk file has manual edits, exits 0 after clean --write regeneration. Validates the idempotent re-generation contract used as a CI lint gate.

100-ci-generate-jenkins-circleci.notes: Behavior proof for workflow#804: wfctl ci generate --platform jenkins/circleci now config-derived via cigen (RenderJenkins/RenderCircleCI). Runs real wfctl, asserts emitted Jenkinsfile/.circleci/config.yml carry secret wiring, wfctl migrations up, smoke, plan-guard, wfctl infra apply, and are free of retired legacy stages (go test / wfctl deploy --image / docker build) per ADR 0044. Requires wfctl >= v0.68.0 (WFCTL_BIN). 22/22 local pass.

103-control-plane-descriptors.notes: Validates committed control-plane descriptor, audit envelope, and registration artifacts through released workflow-plugin-control-plane protobuf validators; rejects raw URL, secret ref, raw actor identifier, and stale provenance fixtures without introducing a runtime endpoint.

104-signal-e2e-encryption.notes: Builds workflow-plugin-signal as an external plugin, launches workflow-server, and drives participant-parametric HTTP APIs for session prepare, direct encrypt/decrypt, queued outbox/inbox envelope delivery, reply decrypt, and custody-attested send readiness. Proves two clients publish bundles, exchange encrypted messages in both directions, move a queued ciphertext envelope through claimed/received states, recover plaintext only at the recipient, produce distinct encrypted envelopes, and obtain host-custody send-preparation evidence through the running Workflow app with no official Signal service egress.

105-encrypted-spaces-proof-workflow.notes: Builds workflow-plugin-encrypted-spaces as an external plugin, launches workflow-server, initializes participant-parametric encrypted-space membership through the running app, persists lifecycle snapshots in a local file-backed encrypted_space.state_store, gates two-member encrypted operation append on positive member checks, rejects unknown and removed members, verifies commitments/proof evidence, removes one member, validates schema/checksum state snapshot metadata, restarts Workflow, and proves the removed member remains rejected after reload. The Workflow app runtime uses local memory operation stores plus local file state, no S3, and no live external service egress; the harness may clone plugin source when no compatible local checkout is available.

106-signal-ratchet-secure-channel.notes: Builds or resolves the real ratchet CLI, creates an acpx.flow-run-bundle.v1 run directory, builds workflow-plugin-signal as an external plugin, launches workflow-server, and drives participant-parametric HTTP APIs for Signal session prepare plus queued outbox/inbox delivery. Proves a Ratchet flow-run bundle descriptor can be sent from one caller-supplied participant to another as Signal ciphertext, that the queue response does not expose the descriptor marker or plaintext, and that the recipient decrypts the descriptor back to the real Ratchet bundle manifest. No official Signal service egress.

107-signal-encrypted-blob.notes: Builds workflow-plugin-signal as an external plugin, launches workflow-server, and drives participant-parametric HTTP APIs for Signal session prepare plus encrypted blob upload/download. Proves sender encrypts blob bytes, a mock object-store boundary persists only encrypted blob JSON and Signal manifest envelope without plaintext/plaintext digest/content key, and recipient decrypts the original blob through a separate Workflow API call. No official Signal service egress or official Signal media upload/download.

108-encrypted-spaces-private-membership.notes: Builds workflow-plugin-encrypted-spaces as an external plugin, launches workflow-server, and drives participant-parametric HTTP APIs for private membership credential issue, presentation, accepted verification, revoked-commitment rejection, and operation-mismatch rejection. Proves two caller-supplied members receive distinct opaque commitments, responses do not expose plaintext member IDs or issuer secret material, and accepted reports remain marked as a local non-official-ZK subset. No official Signal service egress.

109-signal-official-service-dryrun.notes: Builds workflow-plugin-signal v0.14.1 as an external plugin, launches workflow-server, and drives caller-parametric HTTP APIs for approval validation and service submit. Proves incomplete live approval denial, complete no-dry-run denial, complete dry-run acceptance without egress, fake/sandbox accepted operations, challenge handling, scheme-shaped raw credential redaction, and output redaction. No official Signal service egress.

110-signal-public-intake.notes: Builds workflow-plugin-signal v0.15.0 as an external plugin, launches workflow-server, and drives public-intake HTTP APIs for operator bundle publish, unauthenticated public bundle resolve, encrypted message/blob submission, mock queue/object-store ciphertext persistence, wrong-principal decrypt denial, expired/audience-mismatched resolve denial, and authorized operator decrypt. No official Signal service egress.
